activities.title: 🪄 Peekaboo
activities.description: Hide behind a blanket, towel, or your hands and surprise your baby with a joyful "Peekaboo!" They'll love the anticipation and the happy reveal.
activities.tip: 💡 Use different voices for each peek — high, low, silly — to keep it exciting!

activities.title: 🧱 Stacking Cups & Blocks
activities.description: Give your baby chunky plastic cups, soft blocks, or nesting toys. They'll love knocking them down and rebuilding (with your help at first!).
activities.tip: 💡 Start with 3–4 pieces and add more as they get the hang of it.

activities.title: 🪑 Pull-to-Stand Practice
activities.description: Place sturdy furniture (coffee table, couch) at a safe height for your baby to pull themselves up. Stay close for supervision!
activities.tip: 💡 Pad sharp corners and anchor furniture to the wall for safety.

activities.title: 🖐️ Texture Boards
activities.description: Create a board with different fabric swatches, sandpaper, bubble wrap, smooth wood, and more. Let your baby explore every texture!
activities.tip: 💡 Use a small notebook to note which textures they reach for most — it reveals their preferences.

activities.title: 💧 Water Play
activities.description: A shallow tub with warm water and cups, ladles, and floating toys. They'll pour, splash, and discover which objects sink or float.
activities.tip: 💡 Always supervise closely. Add a few drops of food-safe dye for extra visual fun!

activities.title: 🥁 Musical Instruments
activities.description: Pots, wooden spoons, a small xylophone, or shakers made from containers filled with rice or beans. Music + rhythm = pure joy.
activities.tip: 💡 Make shakers together — it's a craft and a toy in one!

activities.title: 📖 Picture Books
activities.description: Thick board books with bright colors and simple pictures. Let them turn the pages themselves — even if it's 3 at once!
activities.tip: 💡 Narrate everything: "Look, a red ball!" — building vocabulary starts early.

activities.title: ⚽ Ball Rolling
activities.description: Sit facing your baby an arm's length apart and roll a soft ball back and forth. A perfect first lesson in turn-taking!
activities.tip: 💡 Use a lightweight foam ball — soft enough even if they bonk their own face.

activities.title: 👏 Copycat Games
activities.description: Clapping, pat-a-cake, waving, and blowing raspberries. Your baby will try to imitate you — and giggle at the results!
activities.tip: 💡 Slow down your movements and repeat the same gestures. Repetition is the key.

activities.title: 📦 Cardboard Boxes
activities.description: Empty tissue boxes, cereal boxes, and shipping cartons are endlessly fascinating. Put things in, dump them out, crawl in — pure wonder.
activities.tip: 💡 Keep a stash of clean boxes in a closet and rotate them every few days for "new" discovery.

activities.title: 🔷 Shape Sorters
activities.description: Simple shape-sorter toys with large holes and chunky pieces. They'll learn through trial and error — and celebrate when it clicks!
activities.tip: 💡 Start with just 2 shapes. Too many at once is frustrating, not fun.

activities.title: 🧩 Chunky Puzzles
activities.description: Puzzles with large knobs for easy grabbing. Pegged pieces are perfect for small hands that haven't mastered the pincer grip yet.
activities.tip: 💡 Choose 2–3 piece puzzles to start. Matching a picture = instant pride!
